message:
fuzz(verify-ext4-integrity): restore OOM regression seed post-fix

The struct_dirty_combined_muts seed (mode=Dirty, focused mutate_byte
of blocks_count low byte to 0xAB, wide round of 9 bytes at SB+0x3D0,
max_inodes=0) was set aside earlier in this session because it OOM'd
the verifier under the 2 GiB RSS budget. With the three hardening
fixes I committed (b9f00e3) — inodes_per_group divide-by-zero guard,
lazy ino_iter (no eager Vec<u32> over inodes_count), and
MAX_GROUPS_VERIFIED cap on the group-descriptor scan — the seed
now runs in 201 ms with peak RSS ~500 MiB.

Restoring it to the corpus pins the regression: any future change
that re-introduces an unbounded allocation proportional to a
fuzz-controlled superblock field will surface immediately as an OOM
on this seed during corpus replay.

Validated: 20-second fuzz run (-rss_limit_mb=2048) reaches ~178
corpus entries with 0 crashes/OOMs.
